_fixture_note: Schema-conformant fixture matching the patched AuditResultSchema + system prompt rules. NOT a live LLM call (sandbox has no model network access). Run locally with `bun run dev` against a real key to confirm live model behavior.
company_info: Seed-stage AI workflow automation company. Founding year insufficient data.
report_card: Product page names specific integrations (Slack, Notion).
Pricing page is public with three tiers.
support_gaps: Performance claims appear without a visible benchmark or source.
Customer-proof claims name no customers on the page.
assumptions_to_test: Assumes buyers accept '10x faster' without a stated baseline.
Assumes 'enterprise-ready' is read as proven, not aspirational.
website_fixes: Add a sourced benchmark behind the '10x faster' claim.
Name or logo-wall at least three real customers.
recommended_next_steps: Publish one methodology note for the performance claim.
Add a security page if 'enterprise-ready' stays on the site.
action_plan: Close the two high-priority support gaps first.

summary.executive_summary: Performance and proof claims outrun the visible evidence. The support gap is the credibility gap for technical buyers.

claims.normalized_claim: Product is ten times faster than manual work.
claims.visible_evidence: No benchmark, baseline, or methodology shown.
claims.support_gap: Quantified speed claim with no stated baseline or source.
claims.evidence_needed: A defined baseline and a repeatable benchmark methodology.
claims.business_impact: Technical buyers discount unquantified speed claims immediately.
claims.trust_gap: An unsourced number reads as marketing, lowering credibility.
claims.positioning_risk: Every competitor claims a speed multiple; this adds no distinction.
claims.safer_framing: Cut [task] from [X] minutes to [Y] in our [defined] benchmark.

claims.visible_evidence: No customer names, logos, or case studies on the page.
claims.support_gap: Customer-proof claim with no named customer.
claims.evidence_needed: Named logos or a case study with a consenting customer.
claims.business_impact: Unnamed proof reads as filler to a buyer doing diligence.
claims.trust_gap: Claiming customers without showing them weakens trust.
claims.positioning_risk: Generic 'leading enterprises' is interchangeable with any vendor.
claims.safer_framing: Used by [Customer A], [Customer B] — see their results.

claims.visible_evidence: Phrase used; no security page or detail linked.
claims.support_gap: Security posture asserted but not described anywhere on site.
claims.evidence_needed: A security page describing actual controls and practices.
claims.business_impact: Procurement and security review need detail the page does not give.
claims.trust_gap: An undocumented security claim invites doubt during review.
claims.positioning_risk: Undifferentiated from any vendor that types the same phrase.
claims.safer_framing: Our security practices: [list] — full details on our security page.

disclaimer: Business and marketing audit only. Not legal, medical, regulatory, or compliance advice. Missing evidence is reported as a visibility gap, not a finding of falsity.
